content: What happened to the people of Israel in Egypt?
options: They were fruitful and multiplied greatly
explanation: Exodus 1:7 — 'But the people of Israel were fruitful and increased greatly; they multiplied and grew exceedingly strong, so that the land was filled with them.'

content: What were the names of the two Hebrew midwives Pharaoh ordered?
explanation: Exodus 1:15 — 'Then the king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives, one of whom was named Shiphrah and the other Puah.'

content: What did Moses' mother place him in to set him afloat on the Nile?
options: A basket of bulrushes daubed with bitumen and pitch
explanation: Exodus 2:3 — 'She took for him a basket made of bulrushes and daubed it with bitumen and pitch. She put the child in it and placed it among the reeds by the river bank.'

content: Pharaoh's daughter named the child Moses because she had drawn him out of the water.
explanation: Exodus 2:10 — 'She named him Moses, because, she said, I drew him out of the water.'

content: What was the name of Moses' wife?
explanation: Exodus 2:21 — 'Moses agreed to stay with the man, and he gave Moses his daughter Zipporah.'

content: In what form did God appear to Moses?
options: A flame of fire in a bush
explanation: Exodus 3:2 — 'The angel of the LORD appeared to him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush.'

content: On which mountain was the burning bush?
explanation: Exodus 3:1 — 'He led his flock to the west side of the wilderness and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.'

content: God said to Moses: 'I AM ___'
explanation: Exodus 3:14 — 'God said to Moses, I AM WHO I AM.' This name reveals God's eternal, self-existent nature.

content: When God asked Moses what was in his hand, what did Moses answer?
explanation: Exodus 4:2 — 'The LORD said to him, What is that in your hand? He said, A staff.'

content: When Moses said he was not eloquent, whom did God send with him?
explanation: Exodus 4:14 — 'The LORD's anger burned against Moses and said, Is there not Aaron, your brother, the Levite? I know that he can speak well.'

content: What did Moses and Aaron say to Pharaoh?
options: Let my people go to worship the LORD
explanation: Exodus 5:1 — 'Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, Let my people go, that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.'

content: After Moses met Pharaoh, Pharaoh ordered that no more straw be supplied to the Israelites for making bricks.
explanation: Exodus 5:7 — 'You shall no longer give the people straw to make bricks, as in the past; let them go and gather straw for themselves.'

content: By what name did God say He had appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob?
explanation: Exodus 6:3 — 'I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, as God Almighty, but by my name the LORD I did not make myself known to them.'

content: What were the names of Moses' parents?
explanation: Exodus 6:20 — 'Amram took as his wife Jochebed his father's sister, and she bore him Aaron and Moses.'

content: When Aaron threw down his staff before Pharaoh, what did it become?
explanation: Exodus 7:10 — 'Aaron cast down his staff before Pharaoh and his servants, and it became a serpent.'

content: What was the first plague God brought on Egypt?
explanation: Exodus 7:20 — 'He lifted up the staff and struck the water in the Nile, in the sight of Pharaoh and in the sight of his servants, and all the water in the Nile turned into blood.'

content: The second plague was frogs covering the whole land of Egypt.
explanation: Exodus 8:6 — 'So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.'

content: What was the third plague brought upon Egypt?
explanation: Exodus 8:16-17 — 'Then the LORD said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, so that it may become gnats in all the land of Egypt.'

content: On what kind of creatures did the plague of pestilence fall?
explanation: Exodus 9:3 — 'The hand of the LORD will fall with a very severe plague upon your livestock that are in the field, the horses, the donkeys, the camels, the herds, and the flocks.'

content: What accompanied the plague of hail?
explanation: Exodus 9:24 — 'There was hail and fire flashing continually in the midst of the hail, very heavy hail, such as had never been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.'

content: What was the eighth plague upon Egypt?
explanation: Exodus 10:4 — 'For if you refuse to let my people go, behold, tomorrow I will bring locusts into your country.'

content: How long did the plague of darkness last over Egypt?
explanation: Exodus 10:22 — 'There was pitch darkness in all the land of Egypt three days.'

content: What was the final plague brought upon Egypt?
explanation: Exodus 11:5 — 'Every firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, even to the firstborn of the slave girl.'

content: At Passover, what animal was each family to slay?
explanation: Exodus 12:3 — 'Every man shall take a lamb.' The Passover lamb is a type of Christ, the Lamb of God.

content: At the priestly ordination, on whose head was the anointing oil poured?
explanation: Exodus 29:7 — 'You shall take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.' Aaron was anointed and set apart for the high priesthood.

content: About how many men of Israel walked out of Egypt?
explanation: Exodus 12:37 — 'The people of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides women and children.'

content: The people of Israel sojourned in Egypt for ___ years.
explanation: Exodus 12:40 — 'The time that the people of Israel lived in Egypt was 430 years.'

content: By what did the LORD lead Israel by day?
explanation: Exodus 13:21 — 'The LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light.'

content: Which sea did God part for the Israelites to cross?
explanation: Exodus 14:21-22 — 'The LORD drove the sea back by a strong east wind all night and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided. And the people of Israel went into the midst of the sea on dry ground.'

content: The LORD used a ___ to drive back the sea all night.
explanation: Exodus 14:21 — 'The LORD drove the sea back by a strong east wind all night.'

content: Who took a tambourine and led the women in song after crossing the Red Sea?
explanation: Exodus 15:20 — 'Then Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a tambourine in her hand, and all the women went out after her with tambourines and dancing.'

content: At Marah, the bitter water became sweet when Moses did what?
options: Struck the water with his staff
Threw a log into the water
explanation: Exodus 15:25 — 'He cried to the LORD, and the LORD showed him a log, and he threw it into the water, and the water became sweet.'

content: What food from heaven did God provide for Israel in the wilderness?
explanation: Exodus 16:15 — 'Moses said to them, It is the bread that the LORD has given you to eat.' This food was called manna.

content: Besides manna, God also provided quail for the Israelites to eat.
explanation: Exodus 16:13 — 'In the evening quail came up and covered the camp, and in the morning dew lay around the camp.'

content: How long did the Israelites eat manna?
explanation: Exodus 16:35 — 'The people of Israel ate the manna forty years, till they came to a habitable land.'

content: At Horeb, what did Moses strike to bring forth water for the people?
explanation: Exodus 17:6 — 'I will stand before you there on the rock at Horeb, and you shall strike the rock, and water shall come out of it, and the people will drink.'

content: In the battle with Amalek, who held up Moses' hands?
explanation: Exodus 17:12 — 'Aaron and Hur held up his hands, one on one side and the other on the other side. So his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.'

content: Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, was the priest of Midian.
explanation: Exodus 18:1 — 'Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for Israel.'

content: Jethro advised Moses to appoint judges over how many people?
options: Chiefs of ten thousands, thousands, hundreds
Chiefs of thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens
explanation: Exodus 18:21 — 'Choose able men from all the people... and place such men over the people as chiefs of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.'

content: The Israelites came to Mount Sinai in the third month after leaving Egypt.
explanation: Exodus 19:1 — 'On the third new moon after the people of Israel had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.'

content: God said: 'You shall be to me a ___ and a holy nation.'
explanation: Exodus 19:6 — 'You shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.' This concept is echoed in 1 Peter 2:9.

content: What is the first of the Ten Commandments?
options: You shall have no other gods before God
explanation: Exodus 20:3 — 'You shall have no other gods before me.'

content: What promise accompanies the commandment 'Honor your father and mother'?
options: Your days may be long in the land
explanation: Exodus 20:12 — 'Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.'

content: From what material was the basin in the Tabernacle court made?
options: Bronze from the mirrors of the women
explanation: Exodus 38:8 — 'He made the basin of bronze and its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the ministering women who ministered in the entrance of the tent of meeting.'

content: How long must a Hebrew slave serve before being set free?
explanation: Exodus 21:2 — 'When you buy a Hebrew slave, he shall serve six years, and in the seventh he shall go out free.'

content: The law of Exodus included 'eye for eye, tooth for tooth.'
explanation: Exodus 21:24 — 'Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.' This is the principle of just retribution in the Mosaic law.

content: The Mosaic law permitted mistreatment of foreigners.
explanation: Exodus 22:21 — 'You shall not wrong a sojourner or oppress him, for you were sojourners in the land of Egypt.'

content: How many great feasts must Israel observe each year?
explanation: Exodus 23:14 — 'Three times in the year you shall keep a feast to me.' These are the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the Feast of Harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering.

content: On what material did God write the Ten Commandments?
explanation: Exodus 24:12 — 'The LORD said to Moses, Come up to me on the mountain and wait there, that I may give you the tablets of stone, with the law and the commandment, which I have written for their instruction.'

content: How long was Moses on Mount Sinai?
explanation: Exodus 24:18 — 'Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.'

content: Of what main material was the Ark of the Covenant made?
options: Acacia wood overlaid with pure gold
explanation: Exodus 25:10-11 — 'They shall make an ark of acacia wood... You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and outside shall you overlay it.'

content: On the mercy seat were two cherubim of gold.
explanation: Exodus 25:18 — 'You shall make two cherubim of gold; of hammered work shall you make them, on the two ends of the mercy seat.'

content: How many curtains of fine linen made up the Tabernacle?
explanation: Exodus 26:1 — 'You shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns; with cherubim skillfully worked into them.'

content: From what was the altar of burnt offering in the Tabernacle made?
explanation: Exodus 27:1-2 — 'You shall make the altar of acacia wood... and you shall overlay it with bronze.'

content: Who was chosen as the first priest of Israel?
explanation: Exodus 28:1 — 'Bring near to you Aaron your brother, and his sons with him, from among the people of Israel, to serve me as priests.'

content: How many precious stones were on the high priest's breastpiece?
explanation: Exodus 28:21 — 'There shall be twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Israel.' The twelve stones represent the twelve tribes.

content: Each Israelite, when numbered in the census, had to pay ___ shekel of silver.
explanation: Exodus 30:13 — 'Each one shall give half a shekel as an offering to the LORD.' This was a ransom for life.

content: The holy anointing oil was compounded from five ingredients including myrrh, cinnamon, calamus, cassia, and olive oil.
explanation: Exodus 30:23-25 — The holy anointing oil contained myrrh, cinnamon, calamus, cassia, and olive oil. This mixture was reserved for sacred use only.

content: Whom did God choose and gift with artistic ability to build the Tabernacle?
explanation: Exodus 31:2-3 — 'I have called by name Bezalel... and I have filled him with the Spirit of God, with ability and intelligence.'

content: The two tablets of the testimony were written by the finger of God.
explanation: Exodus 31:18 — 'When he had finished speaking with him on Mount Sinai, he gave to Moses the two tablets of the testimony, tablets of stone, written with the finger of God.'

content: While Moses was long on the mountain, what idol did Israel cast to worship?
explanation: Exodus 32:4 — 'He received the gold from their hand and fashioned it with a graving tool and made a golden calf.'

content: When Moses saw Israel worshiping the golden calf, what did he do with the two tablets?
options: Broke them at the foot of the mountain
explanation: Exodus 32:19 — 'As soon as he came near the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moses' anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets out of his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.'

content: The LORD spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend.
explanation: Exodus 33:11 — 'Thus the LORD used to speak to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend.'

content: When Moses asked to see God's glory, what did God say?
options: No man shall see my face and live
I will appear in a cloud
explanation: Exodus 33:20 — 'But, he said, you cannot see my face, for man shall not see me and live.'

content: After Moses broke the two tablets, God told him to cut two new tablets of stone.
explanation: Exodus 34:1 — 'The LORD said to Moses, Cut for yourself two tablets of stone like the first, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets.'

content: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai, what happened to his face?
options: His face was covered with a veil
The skin of his face shone
explanation: Exodus 34:29 — 'When Moses came down from Mount Sinai... Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with God.'

content: Moses commanded the people to do no work on the Sabbath day.
explanation: Exodus 35:2 — 'Six days work shall be done, but on the seventh day you shall have a Sabbath of solemn rest, holy to the LORD.'

content: Besides Bezalel, who was the chief assistant in building the Tabernacle?
explanation: Exodus 35:34 — 'And he has inspired him to teach, both him and Oholiab... He has filled them with skill to do every sort of work.'

content: When the Israelites brought too much material for the Tabernacle, what did Moses do?
explanation: Exodus 36:6-7 — 'So Moses gave command, and word was proclaimed throughout the camp, Let no man or woman do anything more for the contribution for the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing.'

content: ___ made the Ark of the Covenant of acacia wood.
explanation: Exodus 37:1 — 'Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood.'

content: On the priestly turban was a plate of gold engraved: '___'
explanation: Exodus 39:30 — 'They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote on it an inscription, like the engraving of a signet, Holy to the LORD.'

content: The Israelites did all the work of the Tabernacle exactly as the LORD had commanded Moses.
explanation: Exodus 39:32 — 'Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was finished, and the people of Israel did according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses.'

content: On the first day of which month was the Tabernacle set up?
explanation: Exodus 40:2 — 'On the first day of the first month you shall erect the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.'

content: When the Tabernacle was finished, what happened?
options: The cloud covered the tent of meeting and the glory of the LORD filled the Tabernacle
explanation: Exodus 40:34 — 'Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.'

content: How did Israel know when to set out on their journeys?
options: By the sound of a trumpet
When the cloud was taken up from over the Tabernacle
explanation: Exodus 40:36-37 — 'Throughout all their journeys, whenever the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the people of Israel would set out. But if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not set out.'
